mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month payroll employment growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, driving positive equity valuations through increased corporate earnings expectations over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137640 thousand as of Dec 2013
regime_hint: post-recession recovery with accelerating labor market
failure_condition: Payroll growth remains below 100,000 persons per month for 3 consecutive months while SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 2.0% represents an accommodative monetary stance relative to inflation expectations. Maintenance of FEDFUNDS below 2.5% over the next 60 trading days sustains low discount rates and positive equity risk premium, producing positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS rises above 2.5% within 60 trading days and SPX produces positive returns, or forward 60-day returns are not significantly higher (t-test p > 0.05) when FEDFUNDS < 2.5% versus when FEDFUNDS >= 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 975 thousand units indicate sustained residential construction demand. Joint acceleration in HOUST and RSAFS (retail sales) signals broad-based consumer and construction sector strength, producing positive equity returns through multiplier effects on corporate earnings over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 975 thousand, RSAFS at 384211 million as of Nov 2013
regime_hint: housing recovery and consumer spending acceleration
failure_condition: Both HOUST and RSAFS decline month-over-month for 2 consecutive months while SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of instances, or interaction coefficient between HOUST and RSAFS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production index at 110.9 reflects capacity utilization and manufacturing output. A month-over-month decline in INDPRO below 110.5 signals weakening supply-side momentum and potential earnings headwinds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as investors reprrice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines > 0.3% month-over-month in 3 consecutive months while SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 50% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 5.5% combined with month-over-month payroll growth signals tightening labor market slack. When UNRATE declines by > 0.2 percentage points over 3 months while PAYEMS grows > 150,000 per month, wage pressure expectations rise, triggering equity repricing downward over 60 trading days as investors anticipate Fed tightening.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE declines > 0.2pp in 3 months AND PAYEMS growth > 150k/month
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 5.5%, PAYEMS at 137640 thousand as of Dec 2013
regime_hint: late-cycle labor market tightening with Fed policy normalization risk
failure_condition: UNRATE declines > 0.2pp and PAYEMS grows > 150k/month for 3 consecutive months while SPX produces negative 60-day returns in < 45% of instances, or interaction term between UNRATE change and PAYEMS growth is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation measured by CPIAUCSL at 215.132 (index 1982-1984=100) is stable. A month-over-month increase in CPIAUCSL > 0.3% combined with FEDFUNDS remaining below 2.5% creates a negative real rate environment that supports equity valuations through reduced real discount rates, producing positive 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 215.132, FEDFUNDS at 2.0% as of Nov-Dec 2013
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with stable inflation expectations
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.3% and FEDFUNDS < 2.5% in 4 consecutive months while SPX produces positive 20-day returns in < 50% of instances, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) at 11703.6 billion chained 2017 dollars represents post-recession expansion. Quarterly GDP growth acceleration above 2.5% annualized combined with sustained RSAFS growth signals robust aggregate demand, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through earnings expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11703.6 billion as of Q3 2013, RSAFS at 384211 million as of Nov 2013
regime_hint: sustained economic expansion with above-trend growth
failure_condition: GDPC1 quarterly growth exceeds 2.5% annualized while SPX produces positive 60-day returns in < 55% of instances, or correlation between GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth is < 0.30 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 110.9 combined with unemployment at 5.5% indicates supply-demand balance near full capacity. When INDPRO growth accelerates > 0.5% month-over-month while UNRATE remains stable or rises, supply constraints bind and inflation expectations rise, triggering negative equity returns over 5 trading days as investors anticipate Fed policy tightening.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO rises > 0.5% month-over-month AND UNRATE stable or rising
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 110.9051, UNRATE at 5.5% as of Nov-Dec 2013
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with supply constraints and inflation risk
failure_condition: INDPRO rises > 0.5% month-over-month while UNRATE stable/rising in 3 consecutive months and SPX produces negative 5-day returns in < 45% of instances, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).
